content: We can borrow the language education definition of 'reflexion' as a vocabulary learning exercise to reframe LLM self-reflection: instead of treating LLM self-reflection as only a logical error-correction step, it can be designed as repeated active 'vocabulary building' for the LLM’s internal dynamic memory, where the agent encodes new contextual nuance for problem-solving schemas just as human students memorize and refine the meaning of 'reflexion' across different usage contexts. This creates a new framework for improving Reflexion agents' long-term problem-solving performance.
